quiz.question:
White has recaptured the knight on f4.

There are some weaknesses in White's camp.

What's the best way to proceed here?

answers.text: We shouldn't trade the rooks,  nor capture the pawn on d3 immediately. We'll keep the f-file occupied and play ...Qb5 to attack White's both weak pawns.

feedback.text: That's right! Trading the rooks is unnecessary here, and capturing the pawn on d3 loses to Rxf8+, and the pawn on h7 is hanging.

distractors.text: White's biggest weakness — the d3-pawn is now free to capture. Our rook will be very active, and If White doesn't trade on f8, we'll double up on the d-file.

feedback.text: If we capture on d3 immediately, White trades on f8 (Rxf8+), and the h7-pawn will be hanging.

distractors.text: We need to trade on f4 first (...Rxf4), so we can capture on d3 next. The d-file will be a big asset in this position, since White cannot easily challenge it.

feedback.text: While we get the d-file, White will get the f-file! After Rd1, our king is suddenly very weak.
